---
title: "Vulkan GPU Backend"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Vulkan GPU Backend}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE
)
```

## Enabling Vulkan GPU

ggmlR auto-detects Vulkan at build time. Install the dependencies before
installing the package:

**Linux (Ubuntu/Debian):**
```bash
sudo apt install libvulkan-dev glslc
```

**Windows:**
Install [Vulkan SDK](https://vulkan.lunarg.com/sdk/home) (sets `VULKAN_SDK`
automatically).

Then install ggmlR:
```{r install, eval = FALSE}
install.packages("ggmlR")
# or force Vulkan:
install.packages("ggmlR", configure.args = "--with-vulkan")
```

## Checking Availability

```{r check}
library(ggmlR)

ggml_vulkan_available()
#> TRUE

ggml_vulkan_status()
#> Vulkan: AVAILABLE
#>   Devices: 1
#>   [0] NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090
#>       Memory: 23.56 GB free / 24.00 GB total
```

## Using GPU in Models

The Keras-like API uses GPU automatically when available:
```{r model}
model <- ggml_model_sequential(input_shape = c(28, 28, 1)) |>
  ggml_layer_conv_2d(filters = 32, kernel_size = 3, activation = "relu") |>
  ggml_layer_flatten() |>
  ggml_layer_dense(units = 10, activation = "softmax")

# backend = "auto" uses GPU if available, CPU otherwise
model <- ggml_compile(model, optimizer = "adamw", loss = "cross_entropy")
```

To force a specific backend:
```{r backend}
model <- ggml_compile(model, optimizer = "adamw",
                      loss = "cross_entropy", backend = "vulkan")
model <- ggml_compile(model, optimizer = "adamw",
                      loss = "cross_entropy", backend = "cpu")
```
